Elon Musk initiated an acquisition of American social media company Twitter, Inc. on April 14, 2022, and concluded it on October 28, 2022. Musk had begun buying shares of the company in January 2022, becoming its largest shareholder by April with a 9.1 percent ownership stake. Twitter invited Musk to join its board of directors, an offer he declined. On April 14, Musk made an unsolicited offer to purchase the company, to which Twitter's board responded with a "poison pill" strategy to resist a hostile takeover before unanimously accepting Musk's buyout offer of $44 billion on April 25. Musk stated that he planned to introduce new features to the platform, make its algorithms open-source, combat spambot accounts, and promote free speech.
Topic Committee: Zach Berg (chair), Eira Prakash, Bridget Zelin, Eesha Gupta (equity)

Resolution:
TH, as a shareholder of Tesla, would vote for Elon Musk's pay package.
In 2018, Tesla shareholders voted for a pay package for Elon Musk that gave him millions of options in Tesla that would be worth $55B if the stock hit certain milestones, many of which seemed like stretch goals at the time.  Despite hitting the milestones years early, a judge nullified the package saying that the shareholder vote (not the pay itself) was illegal.  Tesla is now asking its shareholders to vote on the exact same pay package giving Musk all of his options (because he had hit all the milestones at some point), despite the fact that Tesla's recent stock decline means that milestones equating to approximately 1/3 of those options were "unachieved."

Resolution:
THW replace Sales Tax with Value-Added Tax (VAT) in the United States.
Info: A Value-Added Tax assesses the value of a given product at every stage of development and taxes it based on that value. As the product goes down the production line any added value gets taxed. A VAT is different from a sales tax in that a sales tax is only levied once, at the end of production after the consumer purchases the product. Contrarily the VAT is paid at every stage of production. However, every business along the value chain receives a tax credit for the VAT already paid. The end consumer does not, making it a tax on final consumption.
Topic Committee: Zach Berg (chair), Norah Kiely, Emily Grant, Sophie Rukin, Margot Norman (equity)

Resolution:
THBT the Supreme Court should overturn Chevron Deference.
Under Chevron deference, courts are obligated to defer to experts within the administrative state to make decisions on the interpretations of vague congressional rulings. For example, if Congress passed a law saying the Department of the Interior could create wind farms as long as the environmental impact was less harmful than the possible benefits, the experts within the Department of the Interior would be in charge of conducting the research to find out if the environmental impact was less harmful than the possible benefits. The Courts would then have to defer to whatever the Department of the Interior decided was the smartest environmental/social decision.

Resolution:

The career path, on the other hand, allows you to get a job instantly, but tends to pay a lot less, averaging 30 thousand dollars. Typically, when you choose the career path, it is hard to have the funds to buy a house and pay for things later on in the game.

THP an economy based around worker cooperatives rather than capitalist private enterprises.

Economic globalization refers to the increasing interdependence of world economies resulting from increased cross-border trade of goods and services, integration of international asset trade (stocks, currency, etc.), and widespread transfer of technology/ideas.

THBT developing countries should prioritize preventing monopolistic practices over fast economic growth.
Monopolistic practices are distortions of free market competition by one or more overly powerful companies. For example, if a single private company has exclusive rights to develop all oil fields in a country.

Austerity measures are forms of contractionary economic policy that cut government spending to reduce budget deficit. Typical action includes increasing taxes and cutting social services and government salaries.

Industry-sponsored academic research (as opposed to government or foundation sponsored research) is academic research funded by actors such as corporations, pan-industry organizations, etc.

Resolution:
THR increasing the amount of deposits that are insured by the government at Signature and Silicon Valley Bank.
The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) is an independent agency created by Congress to maintain stability and public confidence in the US financial system.  One of the main things the FDIC does to support its mission is that it insures deposits at member banks up to $250,000 per depositor, per insured bank in the event the bank goes bankrupt or is placed in receivership.  Funding for such insurance comes from charges on member banks, not from general tax revenues. Last month, Silicon Valley Bank and Signature Bank were both placed into receivership after depositors began withdrawing money rapidly.  As an example, deposits at Silicon Valley Bank declined from $173B at the end of 2022, to under $60B in March when the FDIC stepped in.  After taking over both banks, the FDIC announced that all deposits at those banks (only those two banks) would be insured, regardless of the amount.
Topic Committee: Rodda John, Molly Bordoff, Sophie Rukin, Zach Berg, Hannah Riegel, Adrian Turkedjiev, Molly Carroll Thompson

You live in an entirely simulated world until someone named Morpheus goes up to you and offers you an option to learn about the reality of the world. After you have been disillusioned about your situation, a robotically generated agent says he can put you back into the simulation where you will live a life that you always dreamed of and entirely forget the truth of your situation. Resolution:
THR the decline of single combat.
Single combat was a practice in the ancient world through the middle ages wherein two opposing armies would, in place of a full battle, each select one person as their champion, and let the champions fight to the death. The armies would then abide by the results of the duel, with the army whose champion lost surrendering to the army whose champion won.

Resolution:
THP the equal-weight view over the steadfast view of peer-disagreement.
Background: Peer disagreement is when people of similar levels of intelligence and knowledge about a given subject disagree about what is true. For example, if two biologists disagree about how much a duck weighs. How do you resolve this difference? The equal weight view says that one ought to change their view based on what a peer says to meet in the middle. The steadfast view says that one ought to have courage in their conviction and stand behind their view even under disagreement.

Resolution:
“Perhaps no one has yet been truthful enough about what 'truthfulness' is.” 
THP a coherence model of truth to a correspondence model of truth.
The correspondence model of truth holds that a proposition is true if it corresponds to the way the world is.  A valid argument would be: apples are red because I see that they are red, thus ‘apples are red’ is true. The coherence model of truth that a proposition is true if it coheres to existing sets of truths.  The coherence may be internal to a personal set of beliefs that are accessible to a subject. When choosing between two new scientific theories, for example, a coherence thinker is more likely to choose the theory that coheres with existing scientific theories as opposed to choosing the theory that destroys existing thought.

Resolution:
Puritanism was an extreme form of Christianity that emerged in 16th Century England. Dissenting from the Church of England, many Puritans fled to North America - establishing the Massachusetts Bay Colony. Until the very end of the 17th Century, Puritans were the Hegemonic force in the Colony. Church and State were intimately linked in these times - the Puritans believing themselves to be in a covenant with God, seeking to strictly enforce correct moral and religious behavior in order to secure God’s blessing. Puritans believed in the active existence of the Devil and Demons, as well as Witches - persons with supernatural powers gained through collaboration with the Demonic. Seemingly unexplainable phenomena - eg., crop failures, illness, unusually harsh weather - were commonly blamed on the work of Witches.
Instead of staging Witch Trials, This House, as the leadership of a Puritan colonial town in the 1600s Massachusetts, would abstain from the persecution of Witches.

Resolution:
A music label, also known as a record label, is a brand of music recordings and music videos that is owned by a company. The company generally has marketing teams and press teams to promote its artists in addition to publishing their music. Music labels are categorized into major labels and indie labels, with major labels representing 60% to 70% of the music industry. The typical advantages of signing a music label deal include more publicity for an artist and increased access to resources for music production. At the same time, label deals often stipulate a high level of royalties collected by the label company and partial loss of creative control.
THP a world in which music labels do not exist.

Info Slide: In 2017, Teamsters Local 174 went on strike against concrete supplier Glacier Northwest. Consequently, mixed concrete hardened and spoiled. Glacier Northwest sued the union, alleging that the union timed the strike to create intentional property damage. Teamsters Local 174 argued that the strike was federally protected by the National Labor Relations Act, preventing the company from retaliating against the union for striking. The Washington Supreme Court ruled that the company’s claims were preempted under the Supreme Court’s 1959 decision in San Diego Building Trades Council v. Garmon, which blocks the state and municipal regulation of activities protected by federal labor law. The Supreme Court has agreed to review the case and consider whether an employer making common-law allegations of intentional destruction of property during a labor dispute constitutes an exception to federal labor law preemption of state lawsuits.
Resolved: In the case of Glacier Northwest, Inc. v. International Brotherhood of Teamsters Local Union No. 174, the Supreme Court of the United States should rule in favor of Glacier Northwest, Inc.
Topic Committee: Esha Shah, Nick Hansen, Angelique Ronald

Resolution:
Info Slide: Mutually preferred judging is a judge assignment system where debate teams rank judges on a preference scale prior to the tournament. When pairing rounds, rather than randomly assigning judges, Tabroom will evaluate the individually submitted rankings of both teams and attempt to assign the highest-ranked mutually preferred judge to each round. This is done by balancing preference (teams getting judges they rated highly) vs. mutuality (teams getting a judge that they and their opponents rated similarly) alongside tournament constraints.
Resolved: At high school parliamentary debate tournaments, random assignment of judges is preferable to mutually preferred judging.

Resolution:
THBT there should be a mandatory cap and trade program for carbon emissions in the United States.
Cap and trade is a system designed to limit pollution in the atmosphere. The cap places a firm limit on how much large companies are allowed to emit, which is lowered over time. The trade is a market that enables companies to purchase and sell allowances that let them emit only a certain amount, as supply and demand set the price.

Resolution: This House believes that university education should be free.
This House would not allow unvaccinated children to attend schools.
This House would arm teachers.
Assuming feasibility, This House would allow individuals to sell their units of Intelligence Quotient (IQ).
Assuming extraterrestrial life was confirmed to exist, This House would make contact.

The Sorting Hat, in the status quo, sorts individuals based on personality traits or their express wishes into the houses of Hogwarts.
This House believes that the Sorting Hat should be random.
This House would ban animal testing.
This House would abolish zoos.
This House would go vegetarian.
This House believes that all Christmas trees should be artificial.
This House regrets the Elf on the Shelf.
This House prefers White Elephant/Yankee Swap gift exchange to Secret Santa.
This House promotes a world where Karmic reincarnation exists.
This House regrets the celebration of athletes with a win-at-all-costs mindset.
This House supports Tiger Parenting.

THS right to work.
“Right to work” refers to laws that prohibit obligatory union membership for certain professions (i.e. without right to work laws, employees of a unionized company are obligated to join the union and pay dues).

THP strong trade unions (e.g. the union negotiating with the company to raise wages) over direct government intervention in labor practices (e.g. the federal government setting rules on workplace conditions).
TH, as the CEO of Starbucks, W recognize a Starbucks workers’ union.
THW replace the current college admissions process to be a lottery-based admissions system.

THR charter schools.
A charter school is a school funded through public funds (taxation) but is operated by a private company.  They are open to all students and do not charge tuition.
TH, as the USFG, W tax US universities with over 100 million dollar endowments as for-profit corporations.

Assuming a tradeoff exists, THBT political parties should prioritize spending on local campaigns (e.g. mayor, state representative, city council etc) instead of campaigns for federal office (e.g. House of Representatives, Senate, presidency etc).

Assuming a tradeoff exists, THBT governments should prioritize geoengineering over limiting carbon emissions.
Geoengineering refers to altering the earth’s climate (e.g. making clouds, placing reflectors in orbit around the earth, etc) in order to make human life sustainable and comfortable despite climate change.

THR urban rent control.
Rent control refers to local policies obligating certain %s of apartments be available at below market rates as low-income housing.
THBT Facebook should apply their third-party fact checking algorithms to politicians.
TH, as a parent, W not let their children use smartphones until the age of 14.

THR the US patent system.
The US patent system grants exclusive rights to inventors for 20 years (i.e. another person cannot use that invention to make money for 20 years).

TH, as the observer in the Trolly Problem, W pull the lever.
The “observer” is the individual depicted as standing at the lever.  If the observer does nothing, the 5 people will die, if the observer pulls the lever, 1 person will die as opposed to the 5.
Note: info slide includes a picture of the trolley problem

Resolution:
THP a compulsory organ donation system to an opt-in system (caveating religious exceptions).
The status quo in the US is an opt-in system whereby when getting a driver’s license, individuals can choose to be organ donors upon their death.  A compulsory system is one where individuals are obligated to give their organs upon their death (assuming medical need), with reasonable exceptions made for religious reasons.
THBT forgeries should not be considered art.
TH prefers a criminal justice system that pursues criminal and civil charges against individuals within companies, rather than fines against the entire company.
On balance, THBT social justice movements are aided by social media platforms.

THW, as the British museum, give back the Elgin Marbles.
The Elgin Marbles are a collection of Greek marble sculptures. Originally in the Parthenon, they are now on display at the British museum, having been taken by the Earl of Elgin in the early 1800s. Thought of as a great cultural artifact of ancient Greece, recent decades have been marked by renewed attempts from the Greek government (and other actors) to demand a return of the Elgin Marbles so they can be restored to the Parthenon.
THR superhero movies.
THR the myth of Santa Claus.
THR the narrative that good triumphs over evil in children’s fairy tales.
THR the invention of the wheel.

THP a world where only one language has ever existed.
This language would be a common language that shares no characteristics with any known language, past or present
THR agriculture.
Assuming we have discovered less advanced extraterrestrial alien life, THW avoid all contact and keep our existence a secret unless/until they have discovered space travel themselves.

THP a world with Vulcan Stoic Philosophy as the dominant narrative.
The guiding principle of Vulcan Stoic Philosophy is: “there is only one way to happiness, and that is to cease worrying about things which are beyond the power of our will.”  This means that you ignore all emotion and prefer hyper rationality.

You are a scientist working at CERN. Working alone one night, you make an accidental breakthrough in your research that will allow humanity to travel faster than the speed of light. You are alone in the lab and the odds of someone else making the same accidental breakthrough are non-existent.
THW destroy the research.
The privatization of Space exploration ought to be preferred over Government funded projects.

You are a train director driving a train down a railway. As you approach the station, you realize that the brakes on your train do not work. However, you notice there is an alternative path on the railway that leads to a different track, where there is only one construction worker sitting on the tracks. There is a switch in your train that, if flipped, would turn your train onto the “alternative” path. If you flipped the switch, you would save the lives of the three construction workers on the main path, but in so doing, would kill the singular construction worker on the “alternative” path.

You wake up in a plain white room. You are seated in a reclining chair with a steel contraption on your head. A woman in a white coat is standing over you. She explains: “The year is 2659. The life with which you are familiar is an experience machine program selected by you some forty years ago. We at IEM interrupt our clients’ programs at ten-year intervals to ensure client satisfaction. Our records indicate that at your three previous interruptions you deemed your program satisfactory and chose to continue. As before, if you choose to continue with your program you will return to your life as you know it with no recollection of this interruption. Your friends, loved ones and projects will all be there. Of course, you may choose to terminate your program at this point if you are unsatisfied for any reason. Do you intend to continue with the program?”

Resolution:
The Fairness Doctrine was a policy under the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) that required television and radio broadcasters, as part of the requirement for broadcasters to hold broadcasting licenses, to devote some of their airtime to discussing controversial matters of public interest and to air contrasting views on those matters in a manner that was honest, equitable, and balanced. However, this did not explicitly require devoting equal time to both sides.

THW implement a ranked-choice voting system in US primary elections.
“A ranked-choice voting system is an electoral system in which voters rank candidates by preference on their ballots. If a candidate wins a majority of first-preference votes, he or she is declared the winner. If no candidate wins a majority of first-preference votes, the candidate with the fewest first-preference votes is eliminated. First-preference votes cast for the failed candidate are eliminated, lifting the second-preference choices indicated on those ballots. A new tally is conducted to determine whether any candidate has won a majority of the adjusted votes. The process is repeated until a candidate wins an outright majority.”

There is a city called Omelas where all the citizens live perfectly fulfilled and happy lives. Buildings are beautiful, crops are plentiful, there is no disease, no crime, and no sorrow of any kind. On the day they reach adulthood, every citizen is taken into the citadel, to a room in the basement. There, in the deepest dungeon, they find a child, malnourished and sick, chained against the wall, in the utmost misery a human being can endure. They know that if the child were comforted, if it were allowed to enjoy a solitary moment of happiness, that the beauty of the city above would crumble, and disease, crime and want would become a part of the peoples lives. For that reason, the child will never be allowed to leave by the city. Every citizen in Omelas knows it is there, and they have all agreed to live with it. Occasionally, a citizen, man or woman, young or old, will walk through the gate on the edge of the city. They will venture into the desert that surrounds the city for as far as can be seen, walk over the horizon, and never be seen or heard from again.  They are known only as The Ones who Walk Away from Omelas.
Motion: THW walk away.

Resolution:
“Cloture” – a vote to end discussion and move to a vote – currently requires 60 votes in the Senate, and is used by minority parties to filibuster (i.e. block legislation) as they only need 41 votes to block a vote on legislation or an appointment. The filibuster has been removed for Judicial and Executive Branch appointments. As a Senate rule, the number of votes needed for cloture can be changed by a simple majority vote.
TH, as a Democratically controlled Senate, would set cloture at a simple majority and end the filibuster.

THP a multi-party system in the US.
A multi-party system is a system with more than 2 political parties.

TH, as Columbia University, would abolish the “Core Curriculum”.
The Core Curriculum is a set of 6 courses, covering literature, philosophy, music, art, science, and writing, with standardized syllabi that focus on Western civilization. Each course is taught in seminar-style classes, and every student must complete the Core Curriculum to graduate.

THP a tiered secondary education model.
A tiered secondary education model is a system which sorts students following junior high school into either a pre-college (a traditional AP / IB program) or vocational / technical training school.
THR assigning grades to high-school students.
THBT the Supreme Court should be abolished.
THR the House Democrats’ decision to impeach Trump.

All newly created states would be admitted to the United States (ie, no debate can happen regarding whether or not they’d be admitted).
TH, as an average High School senior who has been admitted into both Columbia and a flagship state school, would go to the state school.
THS universities divesting from private prison corporations.

TH, as an AP US History teacher, would not use a textbook in the curriculum.
AP US History is a curriculum that covers US History from the European colonization of North America to Reagan’s presidency.
THBT the reconstruction of Notre Dame should be recognizably new instead of a replica of the original.

TH, as Banksy, would reveal your identity.
Banksy is an anonymous England-based street artist, vandal, political activist, and film director, active since the 1990s. His satirical street art and subversive epigrams combine dark humour with graffiti executed in a distinctive stenciling technique.
THBT Maurizio Cattelan’s “Comedian” is not art.

THS ecofeminism.
Ecofeminist analysis explores the connections between women and nature in culture, religion, literature and iconography, and addresses the parallels between the oppression of nature and the oppression of women. These parallels include, but are not limited to: seeing women and nature as property; seeing men as the curators of culture and women as the curators of nature; and how men dominate women and humans dominate nature. Ecofeminism emphasizes that both women and nature must be respected.

THBT the feminist movement should work to define a positive version of masculinity.
A positive version of masculinity is where you must actively do feminist actions as opposed to refraining from doing non-feminist action.
THBT social media has hurt the feminist cause.

THS prescriptivism.
Prescriptivism is the belief that the way that someone speaks can be right or wrong.

THBT in times of crisis that threaten the existence of a community (e.g., famine, war, plague, natural disaster, etc), capable individuals from the community have a moral obligation to dedicate their time directly towards ending the crisis instead of pursuing nonessential activities (e.g., playing sports, making non-propagandist/apolitical art, going on vacation to the beach, pursuing education irrelevant to the crisis, etc.).
THR nation-states.

TH, as a high school student, would delete all of their social media accounts.
By deleting your accounts, you would also delete all past posts, data, metadata, etc.
TH, as a high school student, would just, like, vibe.

TH, as an animal rights organization, would condemn owning pets.

THW abolish pre-trial detention.

TH, as FDR, would use your wheelchair in public.
FDR had a paralytic illness that left him paralyzed from the waist down. This was well-known during his presidency, but he refused to be seen or photographed using his wheelchair. He could walk with assistance and stand with support.

TH, as an omniscient god, would kill Gavrilo Princip before he kills Archduke Franz Ferdinand.
Gavrilo Princip was the individual who killed Archduke Franz Ferdinand — this was the event that is considered the start of World War I.

You are a Roman commander. You just won a civil war against another Roman commander — he has fled to the outskirts of Roman controlled territory.  During this civil war, this commander persecuted your supporters and took their stuff. THW persecute your opponent’s supporters and take their stuff.

Poseidon is the Greek god of the sea, earthquakes, and horses. The Greek faith-tradition is polytheistic.

The Sorting Hat, in the status quo, sorts individuals based on personality traits or their express wishes into the houses of Hogwarts.

THBT Gwen Stefani’s “Hollaback Girl” is better representation for bananas than Raffi’s “Bananaphone.”
“Hollaback Girl” includes a bridge in which the word “bananas” is spelled out four times. “Bananaphone” is a children’s song about a man who calls his friends and family on a banana-shaped phone.

THP banana republics to kangaroo courts.
A banana republic is a small nation dependent on one main source of national income. A kangaroo court is an unofficial court held by a group to hold someone guilty with limited or skewed evidence.
THBT bananas are the best fruit.

There is a city called Omelas where all the citizens live perfectly fulfilled and happy lives. Buildings are beautiful, crops are plentiful, there is no disease, no crime, and no sorrow of any kind. On the day they reach adulthood, every citizen is taken into the citadel, to a room in the basement. There, in the deepest dungeon, they find a child, malnourished and sick, chained against the wall, in the utmost misery a human being can endure. They know that if the child were comforted, if it were allowed to enjoy a solitary moment of happiness, that the beauty of the city above would crumble, and disease, crime and want would become a part of the peoples lives. For that reason, the child will never be allowed to leave by the city. Every citizen in Omelas knows it is there, and they have all agreed to live with it. Occasionally, a citizen, man or woman, young or old, will walk through the gate on the edge of the city. They will venture into the desert that surrounds the city for as far as can be seen, walk over the horizon, and never be seen or heard from again.They are known only as The Ones who Walk Away from Omelas.
Motion: THW walk away.

Information slide: Music streaming is a service in which users can listen to a library of music without buying individual songs or albums. Each time a user listens to a song via the service, streaming companies pay royalties to record labels or independent artists with rights to that song. Royalties per listen are around $0.00735 (Apple Music), $0.00437 (Spotify), or $0.00133 (Pandora Premium). Users pay streaming companies a subscription fee or, in some cases, use a free version with advertisements and limited features.
Motion: This house regrets the rise of music streaming services.
Topic Committee: Yale Debate Association

Resolution:
Information slide: In recent years, Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro has weakened democratic institutions in the country (e.g. he has censored media, imprisoned some political opponents, and filled courts with his supporters). Meanwhile, Venezuela has faced increasing economic difficulty and violence, with many struggling to access basic resources such as food and medicine. On January 10, 2019, Juan Guaidó, the President of the National Assembly (a legislative body) and member of the opposition, challenged Maduro’s claim to the Presidency. 54 countries, including the U.S. and most of South America, recognize Guaidó as Venezuela’s legitimate President, but Maduro continues to exercise power.
Motion: This house believes that the international community should intervene in Venezuela.
Topic Committee: Yale Debate Association

Resolution:
Information Slide: The National Institutes of Health (NIH) is a federal government agency that invests $39.2 billion annually in medical research for the American people. The NIH is funded using taxpayer dollars, and its budget is set by Congress. Most of the $39.2 billion is awarded via competitive grants to research academics applying for research funding. Research priorities and funding allocation are determined by decision-makers who evaluate current opportunities and public health needs. In some cases, this leads to funding rates that do not correlate with US burdens of disease. For example, HIV/AIDS receives the 2nd largest amount of funding but is 45th of 75 in US disease burden. By contrast, coronary heart disease is 5th in US disease burden but receives the 18th largest amount of funding.
Motion: This house believes that the NIH should apportion research funds proportional to the US burden of disease.
Topic Committee: Yale Debate Association

Resolution:
Information:  In American politics, Democratic Party superdelegates include distinguished party leaders, and elected officials, including all Democratic members of the House and Senate and sitting Democratic governors. Democratic superdelegates are free to support any candidate for the nomination. This contrasts with convention "pledged" delegates who are selected based on the party primaries and caucuses in each U.S. state, in which voters choose among candidates for the party's presidential nomination. Because they are free to support anyone they want, superdelegates could potentially swing the results to nominate a presidential candidate who did not receive the majority of votes during the primaries.
This House would abolish superdelegates.
Topic Committee: Yale Debate Association

Resolution:
THW bring back the Fairness Doctrine.
The Fairness Doctrine was a FCC (Federal Communications Commission) rule which stated broadcasters (such as TV or radio stations) had to cover controversial topics which were important to Americans. Also, broadcasters had to report on both sides of these controversial issues equally. Finally, the rule said anyone attacked on TV or radio had to be given an opportunity to respond to the attack on air.

Resolution:
In 1954 the United States Supreme Court ruled that racial segregation in public schools was unconstitutional, ending segregationist practices that had persisted in parts of the U.S., including Arkansas, since the end of the Civil War.  The Governor of Arkansas threatened to defy this decision using the National Guard and State law enforcement, leading to a stand-off with the Federal Government in which President Eisenhower promised Marine guards to any student agreeing to forcibly integrate the all-white Little Rock Central High School for the 1957-58 school year.  As a Black parent living in Little Rock, Arkansas, in 1957 with no supernatural knowledge of the outcome,
THBT you should volunteer your child to forcibly integrate the high school.
